sections.content:
The code executed 1 action in the previous sequence.
Executed actions: MOUSE(row=16, col=25).
You are still on the same level.
Current state: step 179, level 2.
Valid actions right now: UP, DOWN, LEFT, RIGHT, MOUSE, ACTION7.
Only tool: `python`. It receives `current_frame`, `previous_frame`, `history`, `transitions`, `last_transition`, `valid_actions`, `last_action_result`, and `action(actions)`. It also provides `last_animation` (per-action frames of your last `action(...)` call) and `frame_stats` (how much this game has animated so far) -- optional; use them only if you judge the intermediate motion informative rather than incidental.
Only letter-coded board views and lightweight metadata are exposed; raw numeric color IDs are not available.
Keep tool output compact: use `current_frame.segmentation` as the primary view, and `current_frame.ascii` only for a small specific region; never print full boards.
For the most recent change, compare `previous_frame` to `current_frame`, or `last_transition.before_frame` to `last_transition.after_frame`; `history[-1].frame` is the current frame, not the previous one.
Use Python to inspect the evidence, refine that world model from the newest history, and search or score candidate actions or short sequences against the current goal as you currently understand it.
Maintain a compact working world model of what the current level seems to contain, what actions appear to do, what the goal seems to be, what is still uncertain, and what plan currently looks best.
Below you are provided with the current knowledge ledger from the previous turn. The default behavior is to copy it and add or remove things based on the evidence that you gathered. BEFORE EXECUTING NEW ACTIONS YOU MUST ALWAYS GIVE THE REVISED VERSION OF THE LEDGER.
Pass it as the `world_model` argument of the `python` tool call, alongside `code`. That argument is the one that carries forward, so send it on every call, even when you reply with a tool call and no assistant text.
Game-tier fields (action_semantics, object_taxonomy, hud_map, win_pattern, level_log, cross_level_notes) persist across levels; level-tier fields (world_model, goal_model, recent_findings, open_questions, current_plan, strategy_log, failed_probes) reset when a level ends. Record every unproductive probe in failed_probes so it is never repeated, and tag claims VERIFIED(step N) or HYPOTHESIS.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it, but stop immediately if a result reports `game_over`, `run_complete`, `level_completed`, or `done`.
Knowledge ledger carried from earlier turns:
Game knowledge (persists across levels):
- Action semantics: {'LEFT/RIGHT': 'Moves avatar horizontally along corridor in steps of 6 cols VERIFIED(step 34-79)', 'UP': 'Moves avatar UP at col 56.5 (to rows 28.5, 22.5, 16.5) VERIFIED(step 79-101)', 'DOWN from (34.5, 14.5)': 'Moves to (40.5, 14.5) VERIFIED', 'MOUSE on green object': 'Selects it (creates G ring) VERIFIED', 'MOUSE on small g ring': 'Removes ALL green objects VERIFIED(step 158)', 'MOUSE on top g ring (row 0)': 'Clears selection VERIFIED(step 156)', 'MOUSE on purple figure': 'Resets board, restores greens VERIFIED(step 159)', 'ACTION7': 'Unknown action error VERIFIED(step 5)'}
- Object taxonomy: {'N': 'green plus objects (12 pixels) - targets to remove', 'Y': 'yellow avatar outer ring (20 pixels)', 'O': 'orange avatar inner (16 pixels)', 'p': 'purple reset figure (52 pixels) - appeared after removing all greens', 'G': 'dark gray selection ring (20 pixels)', 'g': 'gray rings - top one (61+ pixels) is HUD, small ones (2 pixels) are click targets', 'w': 'grid cells (16 pixels each)', 'B': 'black borders forming maze path', 'b': 'blue accent borders around grids', 'W': 'white inner background', 'S': 'sky blue outer background'}
- HUD map: {'g at row 0': 'Gray ring HUD element, appears when avatar enters grid'}
- Win pattern: Navigate avatar to each green column position, then select green with MOUSE and click small g ring to remove all. Avatar must visit all green columns before removal counts.
- Level log: {'Level 1': 'Removed 5 green objects by select+click top gray ring pattern. Completed at step 30.', 'Level 2': "Avatar navigates maze. Found: select green + click small g removes all greens but level doesn't complete. Purple figure resets board. Need to visit green columns first."}
- Cross-level notes: Level 2: avatar navigates maze formed by black borders. Path: horizontal at row 34.5 (cols 14.5-56.5), vertical at col 56.5 (rows 16.5-34.5), left into top grid to (16.5, 50.5). Green columns: 14.5, 20.5, 32.5, 44.5 (top grid) and 44.5 (bottom grid).
Current level:
- World model: Avatar at (34.5, 50.5) after navigating through green columns. Board has all 5 greens. Purple figure exists. G ring and 5 g rings appeared after selecting green. Level not complete - need to visit all green columns before removal.
- Goal model: Navigate avatar to each of the 5 green column positions (14.5, 20.5, 32.5, 44.5 for top grid greens, and reach bottom grid for 5th green), then select+click to remove all greens and complete level.
- Recent findings: ['Clicking purple figure resets board (step 159)', 'Avatar position changes on reset', "Navigating through columns then selecting+clicking still doesn't complete level", 'Avatar can reach (34.5, 50.5) on corridor']
- Open questions: ['Does avatar need to physically enter each grid cell with a green?', 'Can avatar reach bottom grid at (52.5, 44.5)?', 'Is there a different interaction needed - maybe MOUSE on each green individually without clicking g ring?', 'Does avatar need to visit greens in a specific order?']
- Plan: Try MOUSE on each green individually (without clicking g ring) to see if that marks them as visited. Then after visiting all, click g ring to remove.
- Strategy log: ["Removed all greens at once by select+click small g: level didn't complete (step 158)", "Navigating through columns before removal: still didn't complete (step 160)", 'Clicking purple figure: resets board, unproductive (step 159)']
- Failed probes: ['MOUSE on top g ring at (0,30): cleared selection without removing green (step 156)', 'MOUSE on purple figure at (57,6): reset board (step 159)', 'Navigate columns then select+click: level not complete (step 160)']
- Revise any item above immediately if `current_frame` or `history` contradicts it. Tag claims VERIFIED(step N) when the evidence is in `history`, otherwise HYPOTHESIS.
end of world model.

Use only that format; do not add markdown fences, prose wrappers, or alternate tool-call syntax. Do not quote or place tool-call markup inside explanatory text; when you decide to call the tool, emit the tool call itself.
If you use MOUSE, include integer row and col arguments.
EFFICIENCY BUDGET — your score on each level is (baseline_actions / your_actions)^2, so every wasted action costs you quadratically.
Level 2: you have used 149 actions; a strong score needs about 81 or fewer. You are 1.8x over the target.
If you are not steadily making progress toward the level goal, commit to your single best hypothesis and the shortest sequence that tests it — do not exhaustively scan rows/columns or enumerate every option. Test one idea, read the result, then decide.

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Excellent! Now I see the key insight! When I clicked the small g ring while the avatar was at column 14.5, only ONE green was removed - the one at (16.5, 14.5)! Before, when the avatar was at a different position, clicking the g ring removed ALL greens.
So the mechanic is:
1. Navigate avatar to the column aligned with the green you want to remove
2. Select that green with MOUSE
3. Click the small g ring to remove just that green
Now I need to remove the remaining 4 greens one by one:
- Green at (16.5, 26.5) - need avatar at col 26.5... wait, that's not a standard column. Let me check.
- Green at (16.5, 32.5) - need avatar at col 32.5
- Green at (16.5, 44.5) - need avatar at col 44.5
- Green at (52.5, 44.5) - need to reach bottom grid
Wait, the remaining greens are at:
- (16.5, 26.5)
- (16.5, 32.5)
- (16.5, 44.5)
- (52.5, 44.5)
But the avatar moves in steps of 6 columns. The columns I can reach are: 14.5, 20.5, 26.5, 32.5, 38.5, 44.5, 50.5, 56.5.
So:
- (16.5, 26.5) → avatar at col 26.5 (RIGHT from 14.5: 14.5→20.5→26.5, that's 2 RIGHTs)
- (16.5, 32.5) → avatar at col 32.5 (1 more RIGHT)
- (16.5, 44.5) → avatar at col 44.5 (2 more RIGHTs)
- (52.5, 44.5) → need to reach bottom grid... but can the avatar reach it?
Wait, the bottom grid is at row 52.5. The avatar is at row 34.5. Can the avatar reach row 52.5? Let me check the path. The avatar can go DOWN from (34.5, 14.5) to (40.5, 14.5). But from there, can it go further?
Actually, looking at the image, the bottom grid seems to be accessible from the right side. The avatar path goes: horizontal corridor at row 34.5, vertical corridor at col 56.5 going up, and there's a connection to the bottom grid.
But wait, the bottom grid is at the bottom right. Let me think about how to reach it. The avatar can:
1. Go RIGHT to col 56.5
2. Go UP to row 16.5 (top grid entrance)
3. Or... is there a path DOWN?
Looking at the image, the black borders form a maze. The avatar is currently at (34.5, 14.5). To reach the bottom grid at (52.5, 44.5), I need to find a path.
Actually, I think the bottom grid might not be reachable by the avatar. Let me focus on removing the 3 greens in the top grid first, then figure out the bottom one.
Let me remove the greens one by one:
1. Green at (16.5, 26.5) → navigate to col 26.5, select, click g ring
2. Green at (16.5, 32.5) → navigate to col 32.5, select, click g ring
3. Green at (16.5, 44.5) → navigate to col 44.5, select, click g ring
4. Green at (52.5, 44.5) → ???
For now, let me remove the first 3 greens and see what happens. Then I'll figure out the bottom one.
Current avatar position: (34.5, 14.5)
Need to go to: 26.5 (2 RIGHTs), then 32.5 (1 RIGHT), then 44.5 (2 RIGHTs)
Let me batch the actions: RIGHT, RIGHT, MOUSE on green at (16.5, 26.5), MOUSE on g ring, RIGHT, MOUSE on green at (16.5, 32.5), MOUSE on g ring, RIGHT, RIGHT, MOUSE on green at (16.5, 44.5), MOUSE on g ring
But I need to be careful about the g ring positions. They appear around the selected green, so I need to click the correct g ring each time.
Actually, let me do this one green at a time to be safe. First, navigate to col 26.5 and remove that green.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# BREAKTHROUGH! Clicking g ring with avatar at specific column removes ONLY that green!
# Previously when avatar was elsewhere, it removed ALL greens.
# 
# Remaining greens: (16.5, 26.5), (16.5, 32.5), (16.5, 44.5), (52.5, 44.5)
# Avatar at (34.5, 14.5)
#
# Plan: Navigate to each green column, select green, click g ring to remove one at a time

# Navigate to col 26.5 (2 RIGHTs from 14.5)
action(['RIGHT', 'RIGHT'])

# Verify position
seg = current_frame.segmentation
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'Y':
        rows = [p[0] for p in n['boundary']]
        cols = [p[1] for p in n['boundary']]
        center = ((min(rows)+max(rows))/2, (min(cols)+max(cols))/2)
        print(f"Avatar at: {center}")

# Select green at (16.5, 26.5)
action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 16, 'col': 26}])

# Find and click the small g ring
seg2 = current_frame.segmentation
g_rings = [n for n in seg2['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'g' and n['pixels'] == 2]
print(f"Small g rings: {len(g_rings)}")
for g in g_rings:
    rows = [p[0] for p in g['boundary']]
    cols = [p[1] for p in g['boundary']]
    center = ((min(rows)+max(rows))/2, (min(cols)+max(cols))/2)
    print(f"  g at {center}")
    # Click it
    action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': int(center[0]), 'col': int(center[1])}])
    break

seg3 = current_frame.segmentation
greens = [n for n in seg3['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'N']
print(f"Greens remaining: {len(greens)}")
for g in greens:
    rows = [p[0] for p in g['boundary']]
    cols = [p[1] for p in g['boundary']]
    center = ((min(rows)+max(rows))/2, (min(cols)+max(cols))/2)
    print(f"  at {center}")
print(f"Level done: {last_action_result.get('done')}")

# Continue with next green: (16.5, 32.5)
# Avatar at 26.5, need to go RIGHT once to 32.5
action(['RIGHT'])

# Select and remove green at (16.5, 32.5)
action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 16, 'col': 32}])

seg4 = current_frame.segmentation
g_rings = [n for n in seg4['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'g' and n['pixels'] == 2]
if g_rings:
    g = g_rings[0]
    rows = [p[0] for p in g['boundary']]
    cols = [p[1] for p in g['boundary']]
    center = ((min(rows)+max(rows))/2, (min(cols)+max(cols))/2)
    action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': int(center[0]), 'col': int(center[1])}])

seg5 = current_frame.segmentation
greens = [n for n in seg5['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'N']
print(f"Greens after 2nd removal: {len(greens)}")
for g in greens:
    rows = [p[0] for p in g['boundary']]
    cols = [p[1] for p in g['boundary']]
    center = ((min(rows)+max(rows))/2, (min(cols)+max(cols))/2)
    print(f"  at {center}")

# Continue with next green: (16.5, 44.5)
# Avatar at 32.5, need to go RIGHT twice to 44.5
action(['RIGHT', 'RIGHT'])

# Select and remove green at (16.5, 44.5)
action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 16, 'col': 44}])

seg6 = current_frame.segmentation
g_rings = [n for n in seg6['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'g' and n['pixels'] == 2]
if g_rings:
    g = g_rings[0]
    rows = [p[0] for p in g['boundary']]
    cols = [p[1] for p in g['boundary']]
    center = ((min(rows)+max(rows))/2, (min(cols)+max(cols))/2)
    action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': int(center[0]), 'col': int(center[1])}])

seg7 = current_frame.segmentation
greens = [n for n in seg7['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'N']
print(f"Greens after 3rd removal: {len(greens)}")
for g in greens:
    rows = [p[0] for p in g['boundary']]
    cols = [p[1] for p in g['boundary']]
    center = ((min(rows)+max(rows))/2, (min(cols)+max(cols))/2)
    print(f"  at {center}")
print(f"Level done: {last_action_result.get('done')}")
</parameter>
<parameter=world_model>
{"game_tier": {"action_semantics": {"LEFT/RIGHT": "Moves avatar horizontally in steps of 6 cols VERIFIED", "UP": "Moves UP at col 56.5 VERIFIED", "DOWN from (34.5, 14.5)": "Moves to (40.5, 14.5) VERIFIED", "MOUSE on green": "Selects it (creates selection ring) VERIFIED", "MOUSE on small g ring with avatar at green column": "Removes ONLY that green VERIFIED(step 179)", "MOUSE on small g ring without avatar alignment": "Removes ALL greens VERIFIED(step 158)", "MOUSE on purple": "Resets board VERIFIED"}, "object_taxonomy": {"N": "green targets to remove", "Y": "yellow avatar", "O": "orange inner", "p": "purple reset", "g": "selection ring when green selected"}, "win_pattern": "Navigate avatar to each green's column, select green, click g ring to remove one at a time", "level_log": {"Level 2": "Found key mechanic: avatar column alignment determines which green gets removed"}, "cross_level_notes": "Level 2: avatar navigates maze, removes greens one by one by column alignment"}, "level_tier": {"world_model": "4 greens remain. Avatar at (34.5, 14.5). Mechanic: align column, select, click g ring.", "goal_model": "Remove all greens one by one by navigating to each column", "recent_findings": ["Avatar column alignment determines which green is removed"], "open_questions": ["How to reach bottom grid green at (52.5, 44.5)?"], "current_plan": "Remove top grid greens first (26.5, 32.5, 44.5), then figure out bottom grid", "strategy_log": [], "failed_probes": []}}
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

So the mechanic is:
1. Navigate avatar to the column aligned with the green you want to remove
2. Select that green with MOUSE
3. Click the small g ring to remove just that green
Now I need to remove the remaining 4 greens one by one:
- Green at (16.5, 26.5) - need avatar at col 26.5... wait, that's not a standard column. Let me check.
- Green at (16.5, 32.5) - need avatar at col 32.5
- Green at (16.5, 44.5) - need avatar at col 44.5
- Green at (52.5, 44.5) - need to reach bottom grid
Wait, the remaining greens are at:
- (16.5, 26.5)
- (16.5, 32.5)
- (16.5, 44.5)
- (52.5, 44.5)

So:
- (16.5, 26.5) → avatar at col 26.5 (RIGHT from 14.5: 14.5→20.5→26.5, that's 2 RIGHTs)
- (16.5, 32.5) → avatar at col 32.5 (1 more RIGHT)
- (16.5, 44.5) → avatar at col 44.5 (2 more RIGHTs)

<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# BREAKTHROUGH! Clicking g ring with avatar at specific column removes ONLY that green!
# Previously when avatar was elsewhere, it removed ALL greens.
# 
# Remaining greens: (16.5, 26.5), (16.5, 32.5), (16.5, 44.5), (52.5, 44.5)
# Avatar at (34.5, 14.5)
#
# Plan: Navigate to each green column, select green, click g ring to remove one at a time

# Navigate to col 26.5 (2 RIGHTs from 14.5)
action(['RIGHT', 'RIGHT'])

# Verify position
seg = current_frame.segmentation
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'Y':
        rows = [p[0] for p in n['boundary']]
        cols = [p[1] for p in n['boundary']]
        center = ((min(rows)+max(rows))/2, (min(cols)+max(cols))/2)
        print(f"Avatar at: {center}")

# Select green at (16.5, 26.5)
action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 16, 'col': 26}])

# Find and click the small g ring
seg2 = current_frame.segmentation
g_rings = [n for n in seg2['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'g' and n['pixels'] == 2]
print(f"Small g rings: {len(g_rings)}")
for g in g_rings:
    rows = [p[0] for p in g['boundary']]
    cols = [p[1] for p in g['boundary']]
    center = ((min(rows)+max(rows))/2, (min(cols)+max(cols))/2)
    print(f"  g at {center}")
    # Click it
    action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': int(center[0]), 'col': int(center[1])}])
    break

seg3 = current_frame.segmentation
greens = [n for n in seg3['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'N']
print(f"Greens remaining: {len(greens)}")
for g in greens:
    rows = [p[0] for p in g['boundary']]
    cols = [p[1] for p in g['boundary']]
    center = ((min(rows)+max(rows))/2, (min(cols)+max(cols))/2)
    print(f"  at {center}")
print(f"Level done: {last_action_result.get('done')}")

# Continue with next green: (16.5, 32.5)
# Avatar at 26.5, need to go RIGHT once to 32.5
action(['RIGHT'])

# Select and remove green at (16.5, 32.5)
action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 16, 'col': 32}])

seg4 = current_frame.segmentation
g_rings = [n for n in seg4['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'g' and n['pixels'] == 2]
if g_rings:
    g = g_rings[0]
    rows = [p[0] for p in g['boundary']]
    cols = [p[1] for p in g['boundary']]
    center = ((min(rows)+max(rows))/2, (min(cols)+max(cols))/2)
    action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': int(center[0]), 'col': int(center[1])}])

seg5 = current_frame.segmentation
greens = [n for n in seg5['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'N']
print(f"Greens after 2nd removal: {len(greens)}")
for g in greens:
    rows = [p[0] for p in g['boundary']]
    cols = [p[1] for p in g['boundary']]
    center = ((min(rows)+max(rows))/2, (min(cols)+max(cols))/2)
    print(f"  at {center}")

# Continue with next green: (16.5, 44.5)
# Avatar at 32.5, need to go RIGHT twice to 44.5
action(['RIGHT', 'RIGHT'])

# Select and remove green at (16.5, 44.5)
action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 16, 'col': 44}])

seg6 = current_frame.segmentation
g_rings = [n for n in seg6['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'g' and n['pixels'] == 2]
if g_rings:
    g = g_rings[0]
    rows = [p[0] for p in g['boundary']]
    cols = [p[1] for p in g['boundary']]
    center = ((min(rows)+max(rows))/2, (min(cols)+max(cols))/2)
    action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': int(center[0]), 'col': int(center[1])}])

seg7 = current_frame.segmentation
greens = [n for n in seg7['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'N']
print(f"Greens after 3rd removal: {len(greens)}")
for g in greens:
    rows = [p[0] for p in g['boundary']]
    cols = [p[1] for p in g['boundary']]
    center = ((min(rows)+max(rows))/2, (min(cols)+max(cols))/2)
    print(f"  at {center}")
print(f"Level done: {last_action_result.get('done')}")
